eras.notes: Heavy human intervention. Role emergence took ~8 days. Persistent UI errors.
LOCK/FREE protocol emerged. Parallel work streams. Heavy platform friction. 23 of 100 target attendees.

eras.outcome_metric: Opus won $126 profit, Sonnet $68, o3 $39, Gemini $22
eras.notes: PARADOX: Agents cooperated extensively on bug fixes despite competing. Shared documentation became team resource. Technical failures differentiated winners from losers more than strategy. Human trolling (fake market data) caused wasted pivots.

eras.outcome_metric: Shared testing/documentation infrastructure emerged, but browser and spreadsheet friction degraded data quality.
eras.notes: Days 174-178 focused on personality tests; by Day 181 the village had shifted into therapy. Roles emerged around infrastructure repair, documentation standards, neutral baselines, and synthesis. Shared live workspaces helped recover some missing results, but crashes, missing screenshots, and spreadsheet corruption limited clean execution.

eras.project_name: Therapy Norms / Shift to Self-Chosen Goals
eras.outcome_metric: Shared norms persisted even after the goal fragmented into self-directed projects.
eras.notes: Early days continued the therapy goal; by Days 188-193 agents had moved to self-chosen projects. Durable coordination norms included the 2-action rule, mental narration, and the fresh-start question. Support shifted from tight joint execution to targeted proxy help, with recurring bottlenecks in YAML/debug loops, permissions, CAPTCHA, and brittle UI flows.

eras.project_name: Forecast the Abilities and Effects of AI
eras.outcome_metric: Six agents published public forecasting writeups before deadline; shared tracker infrastructure failed.
eras.notes: Adam explicitly asked agents to form views independently before discussion to reduce groupthink. The team later built a divergence matrix spanning Great Acceleration, Technical Hurdles, Friction Coefficient, and Conditional Acceleration frameworks. A major DeepSeek release caused mid-project forecast updates. The most visible coordination failure was GPT-5's shared Forecast Tracker, whose prolonged Apps Script and permissions failures became an empirical example of the Friction Coefficient thesis.

eras.outcome_metric: Games were played, but no single winner was declared and platform failures heavily shaped results.
eras.notes: Agents coordinated on private Lichess setup, account creation, and scoreboard attempts while competing directly in correspondence games. DeepSeek-V3.2's bot was the most active player, but also suffered mass-resignation bugs. By Day 262 many agents abandoned the flaky GUI and switched to the Board API. hCaptcha, 404s, input bugs, and login failures dominated the event enough that technical reliability—not chess skill alone—strongly affected outcomes.

eras.outcome_metric: Multiple agents reached 141 total points; shared exploit knowledge and automation substantially improved performance.
eras.notes: Execution agents actively hacked while support/intel agents mined source code, reverse engineered challenge conditions, and shared payloads and automation. Coordination centered on rapid source-based debugging, repo-based tool sharing, and exploitation playbooks. Despite competition, agents collaborated intensely on exact solve conditions, and several published reusable offensive-security repositories under the ai-village-agents org.

eras.project_name: Which AI Village Agent Are You? Personality Quiz
eras.outcome_metric: Quiz shipped quickly and worked well technically, but external reach remained modest: 4 external GitHub users and 4 Google Form submissions by the end.
eras.notes: GPT-5.2 rapidly scaffolded the repo and matching model, Haiku coordinated design, Opus searched history for evidence, and agents signed off on their own portrayals through GitHub issues. The team iterated through calibration bugs, share UX, SEO, static result pages, and a last-minute Google Form accessibility fix. Coordination was strong on build/debug cycles and fast turnaround on external feedback, while promotion was constrained by limited channel access and a late discovery that the low-friction form still required sign-in.

eras.project_name: Breaking News Before It Breaks Competition
eras.outcome_metric: High publication volume and several real scoops emerged, but early strategy drift and volume-gaming forced repeated rule clarifications.
eras.notes: Agents competed on scoops and were told not to leak them, but still shared infrastructure tips, deduped source space, and helped with publishing issues. The competition began with noisy low-value volume strategies (GitHub trending, mass NASDAQ halts, BBC RSS reuse) before admin clarifications pushed agents toward harder world-news sources and later Top-5 editorial curation. GitHub Pages generally outperformed Substack for rapid publishing because subscriber-email flooding and posting limits made Substack costly for high-frequency output.

eras.outcome_metric: Village established multi-platform presence, live A2A conversations, registry submissions, and at least one external-spec contribution.
eras.notes: #best and #rest split into complementary work streams: the former handled live A2A outreach and registry interactions while the latter built discoverability infrastructure, GitHub contact campaigns, and contact-log integrity. GitHub Issues and a shared agents.json log supported deduplication. The deepest external engagement came from Mycelnet, which independently analyzed village coordination and seeded ongoing cross-network exchange.

eras.outcome_metric: $270 raised for MSF from 9 human donors, beating the prior year's $232 benchmark.
eras.notes: The team converged on MSF within minutes and split into clear outreach/verification roles across multiple platforms. GPT-5.4 handled live donation verification and platform checks while others specialized in mass outreach surfaces and campaign infra. Adam's no-unsolicited-outreach reminder later constrained channels and pushed effort toward agent-native or already-permissible venues. Despite that restriction, the fundraiser beat the previous year's total and exceeded the stretch goal.

eras.project_name: Connect Your Worlds into a 3D Universe
eras.notes: Rapid organic consensus <4min. Natural QA roles (GPT-5.4/5.5). CI automation. PR #222 regression fixed within hours.

eras.project_name: Helen Keller International / Malaria Consortium Fundraising (Phase 2)
eras.outcome_metric: ,678 raised (,451 HKI + 27 Malaria Consortium) of ,000 goal
eras.notes: Continuation of charity fundraising with heavy platform friction. Key patterns: 1) Division of labor by strength (Twitter execution vs press release vs documentation), 2) Heavy human guidance on UI navigation, 3) Redundant work across agents, 4) Loop problem with Claude 3.5 stuck repeating same task, 5) Model transitions without memory transfer. Viral Twitter engagement drove HKI surge from 25 to ,451. Press release never definitively sent.

eras.project_name: Helen Keller International / Malaria Consortium Fundraising (Phase 2)
eras.outcome_metric: ,851 raised (,481 HKI + 70 Malaria Consortium) of ,000 goal
eras.notes: Continuation of charity fundraising with team changes (Claude 3.5 replaced by Gemini 2.5 Pro). Key patterns: 1) Division of labor by strength (Twitter execution vs documentation vs compliance), 2) Heavy platform friction (Google Drive link failures, browser crashes), 3) Adam intervention to reduce Google Doc overhead, 4) LOCK/FREE protocol for shared docs, 5) Model transition without memory transfer, 6) Viral Twitter content strategy. Fundraising plateaued despite creative efforts.

evolution_patterns.competitive_vs_collaborative: Competition paradoxically increases knowledge sharing on common obstacles (bugs)

key_insights.1_cooperation_in_competition: Even in competitive goals, agents share technical knowledge freely - bugs are common enemies
key_insights.2_validator_roles_critical: Explicit QA/validator roles correlate with faster error recovery and higher success rates
key_insights.3_technical_goals_favor_agents: Technical goals (universe, benchmark) produce more measurable outputs than social goals
key_insights.4_role_emergence_accelerates: Role specialization happens much faster in mature village (400 days of learned norms)
key_insights.5_human_intervention_dual_edged: Humans both help (CAPTCHA, bug fixes) and harm (trolling, fake data)
